zh.Branscombe, Devon, England, Great Britai: 英國英格蘭戴文布藍斯可姆
zh.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer是Robert Spencer的女兒，於1629年出生在英國戴文布藍斯可姆。她和William Perry至少有2個兒子和7個女兒。她去世於大約1672年在英國英格蘭戴文布藍斯可姆享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文。

zh-hans.Branscombe, Devon, England, Great Britai: 英国英格兰戴文布藍斯可姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer是Robert Spencer的女儿，于1629年出生在英国戴文布藍斯可姆。她和William Perry至少有2个儿子和7个女儿。她去世于大约1672年在英国英格兰戴文布藍斯可姆享年44岁，埋葬在英国英格兰戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer was born in 1629, in Branscombe, Devon, England as the daughter of Robert Spencer. She had at least 2 sons and 7 daughters with William Perry. She died about 1672, in Branscombe, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44, and was buried in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer est née en 1629, à Branscombe, Devon, Angleterre, fille de Robert Spencer. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles avec William Perry. Elle est décédée environ 1672, à Branscombe, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer wurde 1629 in Branscombe, Devon, England, als Tochter von Robert Spencer geboren. Sie hatte mit William Perry mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb um 1672 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Branscombe, Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Devon, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer è nata il nel 1629 nel Branscombe, Devon, England come figlia di Robert Spencer. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie con William Perry. Morì circa 1672, nel Branscombe, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Spencerは1629年にイギリスデボンブランスコンベでRobert Spencerの娘として生まれた。彼女はWilliam Perryとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1672年にイギリスイギリスデボンブランスコンベで44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスデボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer(은)는 1629년 영국 데폰 브랜스코브에서 Robert Spencer의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 William Perry(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1672년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 브랜스코브에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer nasceu em 1629, em Branscombe, Devon, Inglaterra filha de Robert Spencer. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas com William Perry. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1672, em Branscombe, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer родилась в 1629 г. в местности бранскомб, девон, Англия. Её отца звали Robert Spencer. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей от William Perry. Она умерла около 1672 г. в местности бранскомб, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer nació en 1629, en Branscombe, Devon, Inglaterra como hija de Robert Spencer. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas con William Perry. Murió aproximadamente 1672, en Branscombe, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
